skill.name: specdd-refactor
skill.description: Guides refactoring in SpecDD projects while preserving behavior defined by active specs and bootstrap rules.
skill.summary: Refactor SpecDD projects while keeping behavior aligned with active specifications.
skill.author: specdd

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak cryptography patterns, but review found only prose and inline file paths in SKILL.md. No executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Treated as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations use Markdown inline code for SpecDD paths and references. They are documentation instructions, not Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear inside Markdown prose and list items. No Ruby code, command invocation, interpolation, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Treated as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The flagged weak cryptography matches occur in plain-language skill description and refactor rules. No hash, cipher, crypto API, or security-sensitive algorithm is implemented.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural-language guidance about preserving behavior and updating specs. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use.

content.value_statement: SpecDD refactors can break behavior when active specifications are ignored. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through spec-aware refactoring steps.
actual_capabilities: Checks that a project has a root .specdd/bootstrap.md before using the workflow.
Directs the assistant to read active bootstrap files in the documented order.
Guides resolution of the effective spec chain for target files or behavior.
Requires write authority before moving or editing files under SpecDD rules.
Emphasizes behavior preservation, dependency boundaries, and public contract stability.
Encourages baseline and follow-up checks around risky refactor steps.
limitations: It applies only to projects that already use SpecDD.
It does not authorize behavior changes during a pure refactor.
It does not provide automated refactoring scripts or test runners.
It depends on accurate and current project specifications.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Module Under Spec Control
use_cases.description: Use the skill to reorganize code while checking the governing specs and preserving public behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Refactor Authority
use_cases.description: Use the skill to confirm which files may be changed before a structural cleanup begins.

use_cases.title: Align Tests and Docs After Structure Changes
use_cases.description: Use the skill to update tests, docs, and specs only when the refactor changes the structure they describe.

prompt_templates.title: Start a SpecDD Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-refactor to refactor this SpecDD project. First confirm the bootstrap chain and the effective spec chain for the target files.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-refactor on this component. Preserve specified behavior, identify write authority, and run practical checks before and after changes.

prompt_templates.title: Move Files Across a Boundary
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-refactor to evaluate this proposed file move. Confirm the governing specs, public contracts, dependencies, and authority before editing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Complex Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use specdd-refactor to review this refactor plan against the active bootstrap and spec chain. Flag behavior changes, boundary violations, and missing verification.

output_examples.input: Refactor the billing adapter without changing behavior.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the target, confirms the active SpecDD bootstrap chain, resolves governing specs, lists preserved contracts, and proposes small verified steps.

output_examples.input: Move shared helpers into a new package.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks write authority, dependency rules, and public path stability before recommending whether the move fits the current specs.

output_examples.input: Update tests after renaming internal files.
output_examples.output: The assistant limits updates to tests and docs affected by the structure change and separates any behavior change for separate authorization.

best_practices: Resolve the bootstrap chain and effective spec chain before planning edits.
Keep refactor steps small and verify behavior after risky changes.
Document any required behavior change as separate from the refactor.
anti_patterns: Using the skill in a project without a root .specdd/bootstrap.md.
Inferring write authority from nearby filenames or naming conventions.
Renaming public symbols or paths when the request only allows refactoring.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It guides refactoring in SpecDD projects while preserving behavior defined by active specifications.

faq.question: Can it be used in non-SpecDD projects?
faq.answer: No. The skill says to stop if the project lacks a root .specdd/bootstrap.md file.

faq.question: Does it allow behavior changes?
faq.answer: No. If behavior must change, the work should stop being treated as a pure refactor.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and recommends practical baseline and follow-up checks.

faq.question: How does it protect project boundaries?
faq.answer: It requires the assistant to resolve governing specs and confirm write authority before edits.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
